feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Cosmos Revealed: The Science and Ethics of Life Beyond Earth
entry.content:
Authors: M Johnsen

Journal: 2026

Abstract: ... planetary phenomena can create pockets of habitability . Each chapter is designed to expand your imagination, grounded in current science and observational evidence, and to show how life might emerge in ways both familiar ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

Alert: (habitable|habitability planet|planetary|world ... (keyword alert)

entry.title: Cosmos Revealed: The Science and Ethics of Life Beyond Earth
entry.content:
Authors: M Johnsen

Journal: 2026

Abstract: ... in exoplanet science. Its powerful infrared instruments allow astronomers to dissect the light passing through an exoplanet's atmosphere ... If we found a single, immortal alien being on Mars that didn't reproduce and didn't evolve, would we have ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

entry.title: Prebiotics for Brain Health: How Your Gut Feeds Your Mind

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: WYGB Matters

Abstract: ... The metabolic activity triggered by prebiotic fermentation stimulates the production of key neurotransmitters. Gut bacteria produce approximately 95% of the body's serotonin (which regulates mood and sleep), significant amounts of GABA (which ...

entry.title: Ulvan acts as a prebiotic polysaccharide enhancing the production of indoles and short-chain fatty acids in vitro: a metabolomics study

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: B Zonfrillo, CMS Ambrosio, N Mulinacci, J Rubert - Food Chemistry, 2026

Abstract: This study investigated the fermentability and prebiotic potential of ulvan, a sulfated polysaccharide from Ulva lactuca L., using an in vitro human colonic fermentation model. Targeted metabolomics quantified SCFA and tryptophan-derived metabolites ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Origin of Life

Alert: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ... (keyword alert)

entry.title: The Structure of Scientific Socialism: Quantum Emergence , Frustration, and the Non-Dual Dialectic
entry.content:
Authors: SB Roy -arXivpreprintarXiv:2603.27649, 2026

Abstract: Classical Marxism and the algebra of revolution were formulated within the ontological constraints of 19th-century Newtonian materialism-a world of discrete, predictable, billiard-ball interactions. However, the 20th-century transitions in physics ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Physical Biology

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Morphogenesis Across n: Overlays, Emergence Thresholds, and Weak Self-Similarity in the Partition Graph
entry.content:
Authors: FB Lyudogovskiy -arXivpreprintarXiv:2603.25917, 2026

Abstract: We study the partition graphs $G_n$ as a growing family of discrete geometric objects and introduce a formal framework for comparing their structures across different levels. The main tool is a family of Ferrers-translation maps \[ T_\tau:G_n\to ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Physical Biology

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Emergence of multiple quasi-ferromagnetic magnon modes induced by strong magnetoelastic coupling in $ TmFeO_3 $ single crystal
entry.content:
Authors: S Manna, F Fuhrmann, O Gomonay, X Ma, H Chen... -arXivpreprintarXiv..., 2026

Abstract: We investigate the magnetization dynamics of $TmFeO_3$ single crystals across the spin-reorientation phase transition using broadband microwave absorption spectroscopy up to 87.5 GHz. Temperature- and magnetic-field-dependent ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Physical Biology

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)

entry.title: Emergence of a molecular quantum liquid in one dimension
entry.content:
Authors: R Parida, B Paul, HS Adsule, D Sen, T Mishra... -arXivpreprintarXiv..., 2026

Abstract: We investigate the fate of a one-dimensional lattice superfluid formed by hard-core bosons, aka `atoms' (alternatively, a free spinless Fermi sea) subjected to nearest-neighbor attractive Hubbard-like interactions only in subgroups of two sites. The system, as ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Physical Biology

Alert: arxiv" + intitle:"emergence (keyword alert)
